title: Genomic DNA Extraction from Wahlenbergia Leaves
author: Diep R R Ganguly

steps.title: Aliquot the required volume of lysis...
steps.instruction: Aliquot the required volume of lysis buffer (550 µL per sample) and add the following: 2-mercaptoethanol (0.5% v/v) RNase A (100-200 µg/mL) Spermidine or spermine (50 µM, optional)

steps.title: Allow samples to cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow samples to cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of chloroform:IAA...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of chloroform:IAA (24:1) and mix thoroughly for 10-15 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes at 20,000 rcf
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes at 20,000 rcf.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1-volume of 3 M Na0Ac (pH 5.2).

steps.title: Centrifuge for 20 min at 20,000 rcf
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 20 min at 20,000 rcf.

steps.title: Add 750 µL of 70% ethanol and mix by inversion
steps.instruction: Add 750 µL of 70% ethanol and mix by inversion.

steps.title: Resuspend DNA pellet in nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Resuspend DNA pellet in nuclease-free water or Tris (10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8).

steps.title: Prepare lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare lysis buffer. A B 1 Reagent [Cf] 2 cetyl trimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) 2% (w/v) 3 NaCl 2 M 4 Tris-Cl (pH 8) 100 mM 5 EDTA (pH 8) 20 mM 6 PVP-40 1% (w/v) Lysis buffer recipe

steps.title: Add 500-1,000 µL of warmed lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 500-1,000 µL of warmed lysis buffer to frozen and ground tissue (500 µL per 50 mg tissue).

steps.title: Mix vigorously for 10-15 seconds and...
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously for 10-15 seconds and ensure that all plant material is in solution.

steps.title: Incubate at 60 ºC for 1-3...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 60 ºC for 1-3 hours with periodic mixing every ~20-30 minutes (or use a benchtop tube rotator or ThermoMixer if available).

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous phase to clean tube (~400 µL)
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous phase to clean tube (~400 µL).

steps.title: Repeat steps 8-11 on the recovered...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 8-11 on the recovered aqueous phase (be more conservative, e.g. ~300 µL).

steps.title: Incubate for 30-60 mins at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30-60 mins at room temperature (1-12 h at -20 °C if using ethanol).

steps.title: Discard supernatant being careful to not disturb the pellet
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant being careful to not disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 9,200 rcf for 10 mins
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 9,200 rcf for 10 mins.

steps.title: Remove as much ethanol as possible...
steps.instruction: Remove as much ethanol as possible by pipetting, then air dry pellet for 2-3 minutes.

steps.title: Determine yield, purity, and size distribution...
steps.instruction: Determine yield, purity, and size distribution of samples using a Nanodrop and gel/capillary electrophoresis.

steps.title: Incubate for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add 1-volume of 2-propanol (or 3-volumes...
steps.instruction: Add 1-volume of 2-propanol (or 3-volumes of ethanol to avoid excess salts if sample volumes permit).

steps.title: Mix by gentle inversion
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle inversion.

steps.title: Warm lysis buffer (60 ºC) and...
steps.instruction: Warm lysis buffer (60 ºC) and mix by gentle inversion to re-dissolve any precipitates.

steps.title: Incubate at 50 °C to help dissolve DNA if needed
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50 °C to help dissolve DNA if needed.

steps.title: Ensure that your plant material has...
steps.instruction: Ensure that your plant material has been ground into a fine powder via mortal and pestle or with a TissueLyser (do not let material thaw during this process).
